pattern: Dynamic programming where dp[i] counts the number of valid arrays whose concatenated digits equal s[0..i-1]; transition looks back at all valid substrings ending at i that form integers in [1, k].
hints: Let dp[i] = number of ways to restore the subarray from s[0..i-1]; dp[0] = 1 as the empty base.
For each position i, try all j < i: if s[j..i-1] represents an integer with no leading zeros and value in [1,k], add dp[j] to dp[i].
Because k has at most 10 digits, the look-back window per position is at most 10 characters, making the total work O(n * log k).

edge_inputs.description: string whose only valid split has no leading zeros
string with leading zero forces zero valid arrays
all digits are 1, many splits possible
number just above k not valid
single character zero which is forbidden (no element can be 0)

stress.description: random digit strings with moderate k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 50))
    # Start with a non-zero digit
    first = rng.randint(1, 9)
    rest = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    s = str(first) + ''.join(map(str, rest))
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    return [s, k]
